sections.paragraph:
μετρίως adv. of μέτριος (Eur., Hdt. et al.; Plut., Tit. Flam. 373 [9, 5] οὐ μ.; UPZ 71, 5 [152 b.c.]; PRyl 150, 9 ὕβρισεν οὐ μετρίως; PGiss 17, 5 οὐ μ.; Sb 4323, 2 οὐ μ.; 2 Macc 15:38; EpArist 197) moderately, somewhat οὐ μ. greatly (Diod S 18, 45, 4; 20, 83, 2; Plut., Mor. 838f; Procop. Soph., Ep. 5; Philo; Jos., Ant. 15, 194; 276; s. μέτριος) Ac 20:12. The litotes=they received no small comfort.—M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 642
